item.title: Quote of the day by Princess Diana: “Carry out a random act of kindness, with no expectation of…”
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: The mystery of a vanishing Prime Minister: Cheviot Beach 1967 disappearance that shocked Australia and remains unsolved to this day
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Mum who blamed another child for killing 19-day-old daughter jailed for life in Scotland
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: ‘Will never happen’: North Korea rejects Quad’s call for denuclearisation
item.description: North Korea has vehemently rejected the Quad's call for its denuclearisation, vowing "never" to give up its nuclear weapons. The foreign ministry accused the grouping of being a US tool and interfering in its affairs. The Quad nations, including India, had reiterated their commitment to Pyongyang's denuclearisation and condemned its weapons programs.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Indian-origin driver jailed for 29 years for strangling sex worker to death in UK
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Meet Mahmud Sadis Buba: Nigerian politician claimed he was 30 until leaked documents revealed he was actually 16
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: What does NEET mean and why is Britain facing a ‘lost generation’ of 1.25m young people?
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Meet Mike Marshall: The former soldier graduating at 69 after leaving school with no qualifications at 15
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 'War left regime desperate for cash': US sanctions authority overseeing Strait of Hormuz as Middle East conflict enters 90th day
item.description: The US has imposed fresh sanctions on Iran's new Strait of Hormuz authority, escalating economic pressure. This follows US strikes on Iranian military sites after drone downings. The sanctions target the agency regulating transit and charging tolls, impacting global oil and gas supplies. Negotiations for a war-ending deal continue amidst heightened tensions.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: LeT turns on its Pakistan masters over recognising Israel
item.dc:creator: Raj Shekhar Jha

item.title: "Superman to Clark Kent": Clavicular heavily trolled after quitting testosterone as viral transformation shocks viewers
item.description: Looksmaxxing influencer Clavicular, whose real name is Braden Peters, faced intense online criticism after appearing noticeably different following his decision to quit testosterone and other substances. The backlash came weeks after a hospitalization linked to a health scare that prompted him to rethink his lifestyle. While critics mocked his reduced physique, many supporters praised his choice to prioritize long-term health and well-being over maintaining an image built around extreme looksmaxxing practices.
item.dc:creator: Barsha Dutta

item.title: "Charlie makes me emotional": Candace Owens revisits old Charlie Kirk post after reaching new YouTube milestone
item.description: Candace Owens announced that her YouTube channel has surpassed 6 million subscribers, up from 1.35 million when her independent show launched. She credited viewers for the growth, noting that the platform expanded without advertising or outside investors. Owens also reflected on an old Charlie Kirk post celebrating one million followers, saying its resurgence made her emotional. The milestone highlights the growing influence of independent digital media and audience-driven content creation.
item.dc:creator: Barsha Dutta

item.title: Why did Adin Ross refuse UFC Freedom 250 appearance despite Donald Trump planning historic White House fight night?
item.description: Adin Ross said he will not attend UFC Freedom 250 at the White House after hearing rumors that Benjamin Netanyahu could be there. Ross shared the decision during a Kick livestream and said he prefers watching from home with his father. Meanwhile, UFC and White House staff are preparing the historic June 16 event, with Dana White and Donald Trump promising a massive crowd and celebration.
item.dc:creator: Sehjal Gupta

item.title: “This is so stupid”: Candace Owens challenges Blake Neff over Charlie Kirk and Erika Kirk TPUSA successor claims
item.description: Candace Owens and Blake Neff clashed again on X after Neff appeared to mock Owens’ possible presidential ambitions. Owens responded by demanding proof that Charlie Kirk appointed Erika Kirk as his successor at TPUSA. Their argument quickly spread online, with supporters from both sides reacting strongly. The controversy also renewed public debate around Erika Kirk’s growing role within Turning Point USA.
item.dc:creator: Sehjal Gupta

item.title: Valorant streamer GrannyGamingz reveals heartbreaking reason behind return to streaming
item.description: A grandmother, GrannyGamingz, is streaming Valorant to fund a headstone for her daughter, Brooke. Brooke, who passed away in 2023, had encouraged her grandmother to become a streamer. GrannyGamingz aims to honor her daughter's memory through her streams. She also seeks legal assistance to share her daughter's story. Viewers are showing strong support for her cause.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy

item.title: Arsenal Thobe for Eid? Zohran Mamdani goes viral in bold football-themed outfit at Eid al-Adha celebration
item.description: Zohran Mamdani marked Eid al-Adha in the Bronx wearing a custom Arsenal-inspired thobe, blending religious tradition with football identity during a public appearance alongside Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez. The outfit, adapted from Arsenal’s away kit, drew attention online for its symbolic fusion of culture, faith and sport. Mamdani’s message on solidarity and public service framed the celebration as both personal expression and political statement in New York.
item.dc:creator: TOI Sports Desk

item.title: From Iran deal ultimatum to Ebola preparedness: Key takeaways from Donald Trump’s cabinet meeting
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Kick streamer DeenTheGreat faces felony charge over yacht party incident
item.description: Kick streamer DeenTheGreat was arrested in Miami after a yacht party fight, facing felony attempted robbery charges. A woman alleged he tried to snatch her phone while she recorded him during an argument, with Ring footage reportedly corroborating the claim. The incident occurred during a livestream with boxer Adrien Broner, leading to Deen's disappearance from the stream and subsequent booking.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy

item.title: From $642 to $4 million: Fired JPMorgan employee wins huge compensation over a food platter
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Matthew Perry’s assistant Kenneth Iwamasa sentenced to 41 months for supplying ketamine to actor
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk
